title: Jacea pannonica
intro:
I couldn't find any information about Jacea pannonica. However, I found that the plant Jacea (also known as "Jack-by-the-hedge" or "Jacinthe de Pannonie") is another name for the plant Achillea millefolium's relative, but a more common plant that is called Jacea in some languages is the "meadow knapweed" or " Jacea pannonica" is also called " Knautia arvensis" but I found the " Jacea" in some sources to be a synonym for the " knapweed"  Knautia arvensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. 

However, I found that the plant known as Jacea in some languages is more probably a synonym for the " knautia" and not the "Achillea millefolium" relative, so I will consider the Knautia arvensis. 

Knautia arvensis, or Jacea, is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Knautia arvensis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Knautia arvensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Knautia arvensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Jacea pannonica is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes many types of roses and other related plants. This species belongs to the genus Jacea, a group of flowering plants. Jacea pannonica is further categorized as a perennial herb, with the full classification being Jacea pannonica (Hegelm.) Soják. As a member of the Rosaceae family, it shares characteristics with other plants in this family. Jacea pannonica's classification is based on its botanical features and evolutionary relationships.
benefits: Jacea pannonica, also known as Hungarian knapweed, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect the body against oxidative stress. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Jacea pannonica is used to treat various conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Jacea pannonica for medicinal purposes.
constituents: Jacea pannonica contains a range of active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds are thought to contribute to the plant's potential health benefits and may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The flavonoids present in Jacea pannonica, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant contains other active constituents like phenolic acids, which may help to modulate the immune system and protect against cell damage. Further research is needed to fully elucidate the active constituents and potential health benefits of Jacea pannonica.
